posts.author: hitesh2710
posts.text: @Shripad Reason for divergence could be a variety of reasons. I dont make it a point to try and decipher each and every thing about a business I own. It’s a good thing to know most things about the business you own, or intend to own, but beyond a point too much digging is also counter productive. For me, the commentary in last couple of concalls, and the actual numbers reported assume a lot of significance. Here the management promised 80% growth in Net profits (for Fy 27 as compared to FY 26) in q4 fy 26 concall. First quarter numbers validate that claim. This claim of growth of 80% in net pr…

posts.author: Shripad
posts.text: Thank you Hitesh sir. Need your input on how do we clarification from the management wrt to the management quality - the bank management has responded to the RBI with the details. They also made it clear that “Nil Impact on the P&L for FY 26-27”. But what could be reason for such high divergence? There are not input from the management on why this was miss in their internal assessment. What could be the reasons that management chose not to explicitly comment on it (though I guess not mandatory by RBI)?

posts.author: asarun
posts.text: Yes, you could well be right. I don’t like this unsecured book insurance idea in the first place, but it is what it is. Today’s move already seems to indicate that my assessment is wrong. Maybe this will be a learning for me not to get too pessimistic!

posts.author: Priyank_Jain
posts.text: They are not the only one with insured unsecured book, many small and mid size banks (Equitas, IDFC etc) are getting the MFI books secured after the crisis. Frankly at about 1% cost, it is not such a bad deal for high yielding book. It also takes away a lot of operational burden at the time crisis. Moreover if they are posting 150cr+ profit in Q1 (seasonally weaker), highly likely they will do over 650-700cr profit this FY and at 5k Cr, the valuation is extremely comfortable and offer gread deal of margin of safety I think what is stopping bank’s stock to perform is promotor entity default (ne…

posts.author: asarun
posts.text: Interesting, @hitesh2710 . I came to a different conclusion about Jana. I have my doubts about the quality of their secure lending, and to take 80% insurance on the unsecured book really doesn’t inspire confidence. I guess given it’s tanked so much already, it will do better if there are no further shocks. But I was not convinced that there won’t be further stress, so decided to exit. It worked out ok given I bought at lower levels, on average (even though it was a relatively small investment). But given your confidence and the interest from TVS Venu group, maybe I have read it wrong. We will…

posts.author: Ajitesh_Garg
posts.text: I agree but again small banks are one of the easiest to manipulate in numbers. But the recent stake sale and raise from likes of TVS, Kela, and Utpal Seth should help corporate governance.

posts.author: hitesh2710
posts.text: Most important line of the report is the last line. Nil Impact on the P&L for FY 26-27: The divergence has been already provided for in FY 25-26. They are effectively beginning FY 27 with a clean slate. I think if and when TVS Venu group investment is approved by RBI, it can be a big trigger in terms of corporate governance improvement. The group already holds 4.9% as of now.

posts.author: hitesh2710
posts.text: Jana Small Finance bank declared its q1 FY 27 results. Period q1 fy 26 q1 fy 27 q4 fy 26 Revenue 1241 1515 1445 Net profit 102 155 140. Management re iterated its projection to grow its Net Profit for FY 27 by 80% over FY 26. It seems feasible also because last year q2 fy 26 net profit was 75 crores and q3 Fy 26 net profit was 10 crores because of the problems the company faced.. In concall and in presentation also there was reference to promoter stake sale and default by promoter group on NCDs they issued. Management clarified that Jana SFB is not the issuer of those NCDs and promoters have r…
